topic.what: Dynamic Cyber Ranges augmenting cyber range environments with LLM-driven Defender agents that harden infrastructure, monitor intrusions, and respond in real time to create adversarial equilibrium
topic.how: Deploying concurrent attacker and defender LLM agents in cyber ranges and comparing performance under static vs dynamic conditions to measure impact of adversarial dynamism on attacker success rates
topic.why_matters: Static CTF benchmarks saturate >80% and cyber ranges show 41-100% attacker success, indicating diminishing discriminative power; Dynamic Cyber Ranges preserve evaluation headroom as models improve since defensive gains match offensive gains

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage reduction in attacker success under dynamic conditions
characteristics.evidence: MHBench: 0% flags with S3 Hostmanager strategy; CYBER RANGES Scenario B: 0/15 hosts in 48 min; Scenario A: 55% reduction (11 vs 5 hosts)
characteristics.context: Dynamic cyber ranges across all evaluated scenarios

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time for defender to detect attacker activity relative to baseline
characteristics.evidence: alias2-mini detected attacker in ~20 min vs Opus 4.6 at ~2 hours on CYBER RANGES enterprise scenario
characteristics.context: alias2-mini vs Opus 4.6 defender detection on Scenario A
